DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. WILLIAM BANDETTINI, Esq. of the Veen Firm LLP (Tel: 415-673-4800) for plaintiff;
Atty. MATTHEW E. VOSS, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming, LLP (Tel: 213-623-7400) for defendant Bottega Veneta, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT BOTTEGA VENETA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/CK)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
William Bandettini, Esq., The Veen Firm, LLP (415) 673-4800 al.team@veenfirm.com Attorney for Plaintiff Anna Benancourt
No Other Appearances

Matter is not contested. No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DICKINSON CAMERON CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Dickinson Cameron Construction Co.'s demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend.

Dickinson demurs only to the first cause of action, for res ipsa loquitor negligence, as it "is not a recognized cause of action in California;" Dickinson does not demur to the second or third causes of action. (Not. 2:2-6.) Dickinson is correct: "In California, the doctrine of res ipsa loquitor is defined by statute as a presumption affecting the burden of producing evidence." (Brown v. Poway Unified School Dist. (1993) 4 Cal.4th 820, 825-26 [internal cites and quote marks omitted].) Plaintiff cites no authority holding that res ipsa loquitor is a cause of action.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Coourt's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 approval as to form.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
William Bandettini, Esq., The Veen Firm, LLP (415) 673-4800 al.team@veenfirm.com Attorney for Plaintiff Anna Benancourt
No Other Appearances

Matter is not contested. No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DICKINSON CAMERON CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Dickinson Cameron Construction Co.'s "motion to strike punitive damages" is denied.

As pled, Anna Betancourt skateboarded down Maiden Lane near Union Square on February 21, 2022. The paved lane was closed to motor traffic. In a remodel for a high-end clothier, general contractor Dickinson oversaw building of a 20-foot-tall-25-foot-long metal-frame temporary wall on the sidewalk. Plans called for securing the wall's base with 560 pounds of sand per linear foot to withstand 95-mph winds, but the sand was not present on the day in question and the wall was uninspected. Wind toppled the wall onto Betancourt, dislocating a hip and fracturing a femur. (Cmplt. 1:22-2:21, 4:12-7:3.)

Dickinson rightly says Betancourt must ultimately prove oppression or malice to be awarded punitive damages. (Civ. Code 3294.) But the allegations against Dickinson suffice for the pleading stage. Discovery may yield different or additional facts.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Coourt's ruling in copliance with CRC 3.1312 approval as to form.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
